raw_consumer_statement:
On XX/XX/year>, I used my American Express credit card to purchase a one-way airline ticket from XXXX for {$510.00}. 

Within XXXX hours of booking, on the same day, I contacted XXXX customer service to cancel the reservation, following their stated instructions for XXXX flights. I did not check in, did not travel, and did not receive any service. XXXX did not issue any refund. They only offered to refund {$33.00} in taxes, which I declined. 

I then disputed the {$510.00} charge with American Express, providing documentation including screenshots of my cancellation attempt and proof that no service was received. American Express denied the dispute, stating that the merchant refunded {$100.00}. This is incorrect. The {$100.00} credit on my account came from an American Express promotional offer ( Spend {$500.00}, get {$100.00} back ), not from XXXX. 

I reopened the dispute and submitted additional documentation clarifying this factual error, along with evidence showing I canceled within XXXX hours and was not a no-show. Despite this, American Express closed the dispute again without addressing the new evidence and repeated the same incorrect conclusion.

American Express has not provided a substantive explanation addressing the factual errors or why the submitted evidence was not considered.
